query: PieterPost MCP complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Troubleshooting MCP Errors
results.content:
### Tool register failed

 Description: The Agent could not start the MCP tool.
 Resolution: Reload the MCP using your platform's method as described above.

### Client closed

 Description: The Agent attempted to start the MCP, but it was already closed or shut down.
 Resolution: Reload the MCP.

### Agent cannot call tool

 Where it appears: In the Agent chat interface.
 Description: The Agent encountered a runtime error while trying to invoke the MCP. This usually means the MCP was invoked with bad input.
 Resolution: Try running the same prompt again. If the issue persists contact support.

### Auth issue [...] ### Auth issue

 Where it appears: In the MCP result panel.
 Description: The MCP failed to authenticate using the current session.
 Resolution:
  1. Log out of the extension.
  2. Log back in to refresh the session and re-authenticate the MCP.

### Tool returned an error

 Where it appears: In the MCP result panel.
 Description: The MCP was invoked successfully, but encountered an internal runtime error.
 Resolution: Reload the MCP.

If issues persist after reloading, consider restarting your IDE.

Need help?  Chat with our support team or email us at (/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#087b7d7878677a7c48607d6c266167) . [...] Inside Cursor Setting -> MCP

  

### VSCode

1. Open the file named `mcp.json` located in your project workspace
2. Inside this file view, locate the Restart button in the editor toolbar or adjacent to the MCP configuration and click it.

In your `mcp.json`

  
   

### Windsurf

1. Go to the Agent chat panel within the IDE interface.
2. Locate the Refresh button (usually in the top right corner of the MCP message area) and click it.

In your agent panel

# Common MCP Errors and How to Resolve Them

  

### MCP is not enabled

 Description: The MCP is not running because it is not enabled, MCP calls will not work.
 Resolution: Enable the MCP as seen in the documentation.

### Tool register failed

results.title: PieterPost MCP for ChatGPT and Claude
results.content:
1

### Draft

Ask your assistant to write a birthday note, thank-you letter, customer follow-up, invite, or postcard message.

2

### Choose recipient

Pick a saved Mailbook contact or provide a postal address in the conversation.

3

### Review

Open the PieterPost review link and check the exact copy, address, format, and image before checkout.

4

### Send

Pay securely in PieterPost or use approved API credits for explicit direct-send workflows.

## Safety and permissions

PieterPost uses OAuth, so your MCP client asks you to connect your PieterPost account before it can use account-specific tools. Checkout links keep review and payment in PieterPost. Direct API-credit sends require the right OAuth scope and a clear confirmation before they run.

Technical MCP docsManage connections [...] LetterPostcardMailbook

PieterPost remote MCP server

# Send letters and postcards through chat.

Connect PieterPost to a compatible MCP client. Draft a message, choose a saved Mailbook contact, create a secure checkout link, and let PieterPost print, stamp, and mail it after you review the details.

## See the MCP flow in action

This walkthrough shows one compatible MCP client. The same PieterPost server URL and OAuth approval flow work across clients that support remote MCP.

## Connect PieterPost to your assistant

Paste this public remote MCP endpoint into ChatGPT, Claude, or another compatible client:

`

Popular AI workspaces use different names for connectors and MCP. Look for remote MCP or custom connector support; availability can vary by plan and product. [...] OpenAI MCP docs

1. 1Open ChatGPT settings and go to Apps & Connectors.
2. 2Create or add a custom connector, name it PieterPost, and paste the MCP server URL.
3. 3Choose OAuth when prompted, scan the available tools, then authorize your PieterPost account.
4. 4Start a chat with PieterPost enabled and ask for a letter, postcard, checkout link, or order update.

Claude logo

### Claude

Use PieterPost as a custom connector in Claude surfaces that support remote MCP servers and OAuth authorization.

Claude connector docs

results.title: Client hangs indefinitely when MCP server returns HTTP 4xx/5xx errors instead of raising exception · Issue #2595 · PrefectHQ/fastmcp · GitHub
results.content:
Problematic Tool Call Request:

`POST /mcp HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json, text/event-stream
Content-Type: application/json
Request body:
{
"jsonrpc": "2.0",
"id": 0,
"method": "initialize",
"params": {...}
}
Response: 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json
mcp-session-id: be55a98fe9f543b2b67b853215090af9
mcp-protocol-version: 2025-06-18`

### Detailed Log Output

`INFO httpx: HTTP Request: POST  "HTTP/1.1 200 OK"
INFO mcp.client.streamable_http: Received session ID: be55a98fe9f543b2b67b853215090af9
INFO mcp.client.streamable_http: Negotiated protocol version: 2025-06-18
INFO httpx: HTTP Request: POST  "HTTP/1.1 202 Accepted"
INFO httpx: HTTP Request: GET  "HTTP/1.1 200 OK"
INFO httpx: HTTP Request: POST  "HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error"` [...] Key Observations from Logs

## Comparison: Working vs Broken Behavior

Just for your information, guys — in my case, when I try the `/mcp` endpoints using the `get_code` tool in VS Code, I encounter a timeout error. Other tools are working fine. Could this be related to a server-side issue?  Anyone finds a solution on this.

`/mcp`
`get_code`Nick Pape

This still seems broken. VSCode 1.103, fresh Figma installation, dev seat, started the MCP, get the toast that it’s running, but then see the following:

VS Code: [...] Figma Desktop App version 125.11.6

still does NOT work:  
```{"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":{"code":-32001,"message":"Invalid sessionId"},"id":null}```

I’m having the issue with MCP in VScode, after authenticating the logs just show `[info] Waiting for server to respond to `initialize` request...` and printing this message constantly.

Seems like the MCP server never initializes in the VSCode even after authentication was successful…

MCP config is:

```

  "figma/mcp-server-guide": {

      "type": "http",

      "url": "

    },

```

Any ideas?

I have same issue

Dila Gurung

For me it started working after upgrading my VSCODE.  I am using Mac Tahoe.

buttercup_420

MCP whoami returns tier: pro, seat: Full, [...] djv
Figmate

Hey All, thanks for following up!   
  
As ​@Tom Reem mentioned previously, when you’re testing using CURL or in the browser, you’ll receive the Invalid sessionId, because you’re not passing in a session ID as a parameter. While MCP clients do allow this, it’s not designed to be used directly. Again, this is an issue on the Cursor side of things. Even if it goes red after a few minutes, it should still work.  
  
Again, for direct one on one support with this, we encourage you to reach out to our support team directly and provide screenshots of exactly where in your MCP clients the errors are popping up: 

Tom Reem
xiao99xiao

This seems like an expired token or auth. I had Claude Code “Reconnect” and things started working again with /mcp.

👍 This works for me.

Schwepps
